projects.title: PWM-Driven Voltage Controlled Oscillator PCB
projects.summary: Designed, simulated, fabricated, and characterized a mixed-signal VCO that converts a control voltage into an oscillator output through a V2I stage, current mirrors, comparator thresholds, latch logic, and a PWM load interface.
highlights: Targeted a 1 kHz oscillator operating point at a 1 V control input using a 0.022 uF timing capacitor.
Built the VCO around an LM317 supply regulator, LM324-based V2I conversion, CD4007 MOS devices, LM2901 comparators, and 74AC00 NAND latch logic.
Measured V2I/current mirror behavior near the 0.10 mA design target across capacitor-voltage sweeps.
Recorded final frequency-versus-control-voltage characterization from 0 V to 5 V; the workbook shows 1.342 at 1.0 V and a high-control plateau around 3.18.

tests.name: LM317 regulator simulation
tests.method: LTspice operating-point validation for the 10 V input to 5 V regulated rail
tests.result: Operating point was found, supporting the regulated VCC block before full-system integration.

tests.name: V2I and current mirror sweep
tests.method: Nested DC sweep over VCTRL, VCAP, and VFB operating states
tests.result: Measured current values stayed near the 0.10 mA design target across much of the capacitor-voltage sweep.

tests.name: Window comparator and latch verification
tests.method: LTspice comparator-threshold simulation with 1 V low and 3 V high references
tests.result: The comparator/latch stage was checked against the threshold behavior used to reverse the capacitor charge direction.

tests.name: Full oscillator transient simulation
tests.method: Integrated LTspice oscillator run including capacitor charge/discharge behavior
tests.result: Integrated simulation converged and was used to inspect the oscillator waveform path before PCB bring-up.

tests.name: Final PWM load integration
tests.method: LTspice full-circuit test with PWM-driven output load
tests.result: Final simulation connected the oscillator output behavior to the PWM load interface.

tests.name: Frequency vs. control-voltage characterization
tests.method: Bench characterization recorded in the final build workbook
tests.result: Recorded values rise from 0 at 0 V to 1.342 at 1.0 V, then approach a plateau around 3.18 at higher control voltages.
